title: Tilt Illusion by Phase
description: This dataset contains EEG recordings from 36 participants performing a visual discrimination task involving a tilt illusion. Participants judged the orientation of a central grating surrounded by an annular grating at ±30 degrees, which induces a perceptual bias. The study investigates the prestimulus EEG correlates of alpha oscillation power and phase on discrimination performance and the magnitude of the tilt illusion effect.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded using a Biosemi Active2 system with 64 scalp electrodes and 6 EOG electrodes during a visual orientation discrimination task. Stimuli were presented for 8.3 ms at 60% contrast. Participants responded using a keyboard. Original data in .bdf format were converted to EEGLab format (.set and .fdt files) for BIDS compliance.

funding_references.funder_name: National Health and Medical Research Council
